title: Inspired by public API lists, I built an open-source trading RSS directory
author: phithanh1230
selftext:
Hey everyone,

I’ve been working on a small open-source project inspired by things like public API directories and “awesome” lists, but focused specifically on trading.

The idea is to build a community-driven directory of RSS feeds for crypto, forex, stocks, and macro — something useful for indie traders, trading devs, and market analysts who rely on structured information and fast access to sources.

It’s meant to stay simple and open:

* curated and categorized feeds across markets
* easy to browse and extend
* built for contributions over time

This is the project: [Github](https://github.com/Phideround/RealMarketFeed)

It’s still early, but the goal is to grow it into a reliable, shared resource — similar in spirit to public API lists, just more focused on the trading/data side.

If you have good sources to add, ideas on how to structure it better, or want to contribute in any way, I’d really appreciate it.

Would be great to build this together.
